dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal emphasizes timeframe bias and avoiding intermediate timeframe traps. Implemented using intraday breakouts (Donchian channels as proxy for breakout levels) with confirmation from daily trend filter (SMA_50). Entry on breakout in direction of daily trend, exit on daily trend reversal. Used dc_upper/dc_lower for breakout detection and sma_50 as daily trend confirmation. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long and short depending on daily trend direction.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal emphasizes timeframe bias and avoiding intermediate timeframe traps. Implemented using intraday breakouts (Donchian channels as proxy for breakout levels) with confirmation from daily trend filter (SMA_50). Entry on breakout in direction of daily trend, exit on daily trend reversal. Used dc_upper/dc_lower for breakout detection and sma_50 as daily trend confirmation. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long and short depending on daily trend direction.

source_rule_text: A trader should adopt a timeframe bias—either very short-term or very long-term—aligned with their natural trading style, and avoid the intermediate timeframe which traps the majority of trend followers in false breakouts.
